foundational.description: God’s dream—expressed through the commissioning vision of Jesus’ final words on earth—is to see every expression of humanity, both individual and corporate, restored by the Spirit of God through the redemptive message of the Gospel. He wants every person to have the opportunity to be experience God’s life and be adopted into the community of the family of God (Mar 16:15). He wants every nation to be able to experience the transformative impact of the kingdom of God in every dimension of its cultural life, shaping every sphere of society. This is a compelling dream; a dream which calls us to love God with all our heart, soul, mind and strength until it has become an experiential reality here on earth. Until then we work and pray as he taught us, “your kingdom come, your will be done on earth as it is in heaven” (Mat 6:10 NIV).

family.description: God is the one who had the original idea of family. His purpose for this sphere is that life might be multiplied and that a God-given destiny for every individual may be established. Family is intended to provide a safe, loving, and nurturing environment for the growth and education of the next generation. God desires that we engage with families, to serve and strengthen them, to see his purposes restored.

economics.description: God’s purpose for this sphere is to release provision and model stewardship. Business is intended to provide needed goods and services and gainful employment opportunities for the community at a fair market price and wage. When stewarded well, this process of creating and distributing wealth should enhance the life of people and glorify the Creator who inspires innovation. Science and technology discover and use God’s natural laws in order to bless all creation by pursuing a higher quality of life, better health and greater stewardship of God’s resources.

government.description: God has appointed government in order to safeguard justice and create a peaceful society. It does this by providing an independent and objective source of arbitration and conflict resolution for society and between nations, ensuring justice and equity for all its citizens. Rulers should exercise delegated authority in order to serve the citizens whom they govern. We must seek to end every form of injustice, corruption and greed; and to promote the values of servanthood, purity and generosity within this sphere.

religion.description: God’s purpose for this sphere is the extension of mercy and reconciliation between those who have had broken relationships – with others and with God. Competing religious worldviews contend for the hearts and minds of men and women everywhere. Without falling into a contentious spirit, we are to enter this marketplace of religious ideas and clearly display why the gospel of Christ is unique. We are to disciple all believers in the whole nature and character of God, and in applying his Word to every aspect of their lives.

education.description: God has designed the educational sphere to be a means of discipleship and multiplication. The purpose should be the transformation of the student rather than the mere transaction of knowledge, that the God-given gifts in every person might be developed to their highest potential in the service of their community.

communication.description: God’s purpose for media is to provide truthful, objective information of importance to the community at large so that citizens can make informed decisions. Through media God desires the transfer of wisdom and the promotion of healthy relationships. Today, with new media technologies emerging all the time, we have more capacity to communicate than ever before. But what are we communicating? Is it bringing life or death? We should engage in media as followers of Jesus to advance the ways of the kingdom of God.

celebration.description: God’s purposes for the sphere of celebration are to strengthen hope and build community. When God’s ideas are embedded in story form, the truth resonates with both heart and mind, maximizing their impact. This sphere provides rest, relaxation, and renewal of the soul through beauty and joy.
